items.headline: How the House Financial Services Committee is taking on tokenization: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: Rep. French Hill, head of the House Financial Services Committee, weighed in on outstanding policy issues in an interview with CoinDesk last month.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Japan's ruling party supports crypto ETF trading, yen-based stablecoins
items.subheadline: Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party said the country should create a legal framework for trading crypto ETFs in a proposal to the finance minister.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.
items.author: Ji Hun Kim

items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.
items.author: Saad Naja

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.
items.author: Michael S. Selig

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: Gary Vaynerchuk says AI is creating the biggest opportunity ever for entrepreneurs
items.subheadline: AI is reshaping business faster than most leaders realize, and Gary Vaynerchuk argues the biggest winners will be creative builders willing to adapt.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: Titan Network hits 4 million devices on its crowdsourced AI-compute network
items.subheadline: Titan Network built its crowdsourced computing infrastructure on a slim $1 million seed round, signing up tech giants like Tencent and Alibaba.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Hyperliquid predicted 80% of an oil market move before traditional exchanges even opened, says TD Securities
items.subheadline: TD Securities says "perpetual futures" are exploding beyond crypto as platforms like Hyperliquid outpace traditional Wall Street exchanges on everything from pre-IPO tech stocks to weekend oil trading.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: U.S. CFTC files request to erase Gemini settlement that it no longer considers fair
items.subheadline: The regulator went back to analyze the 2022 case and decided that it wouldn't have been pursued under current management and practices.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: The legal risks and practical considerations of digital asset blacklisting
items.subheadline: When digital assets are frozen, holders can suddenly be deprived of access to their legitimate assets or income. Here’s what to know to keep your digital holdings safe.
items.author: Galen Kast

items.headline: Sui blockchain suffers another network outage as transactions grind to a halt
items.subheadline: This is the second time this year that the Sui network has suffered an outage.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Hyperliquid is beating ethereum in trading volume on some days as big money rotates, says FalconX
items.subheadline: Institutional investors are ditching range-bound bitcoin and ether for Hyperliquid as the decentralized platform wins over hedge funds with massive liquidity and early access to hot markets, according to Joshua Lim, head of markets at FalconX.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: How Stellar (XLM) became part of DTCC's plan to bring securities onchain
items.subheadline: The blockchain's ties to DTCC stretch back years through Securrency, while Franklin Templeton's BENJI fund helped demonstrate how regulated assets can operate on public networks.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: ADA governance vote kills Cardano Summit 2026, approves smaller TOKEN2049 plan
items.subheadline: The Cardano Foundation's flagship conference was scrapped after a funding proposal failed to secure the two-thirds support required under the blockchain's new governance system.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: Ethereum news: Bitmine (BMNR) slows purchase pace, buying $53 million in ETH
items.subheadline: The Ethereum treasury firm cut weekly purchases by more than 75% after the previous week's 112,000 ETH buying spree.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Saylor's Strategy (MSTR) sold bitcoin (BTC). These crypto treasuries are still buying
items.subheadline: With Strategy breaking its accumulation streak and many peers stepping aside, the list of active digital asset treasuries has narrowed considerably.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Digital asset firm Keyrock plans to acquire BlockFills out of bankruptcy
items.subheadline: The Brussels-based digital asset services firm plans to purchase BlockFills months after the Chicago-based company filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, if a court approves.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: MoneyGram launches stablecoin on Stellar (XLM), joining rush toward digital dollar payments
items.subheadline: MGUSD, issued by Stripe's Bridge, will power services across MoneyGram's global network as stablecoins gain traction in cross-border payments.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: RWA news: Symbiotic aims to make tokenized assets easier to cash out with Liquid Lane
items.subheadline: Liquid Lane aims to solve a key bottleneck that is holding back growth of tokenized funds and credit: long redemption times, Symbiotic cofounder Misha Putiatin said.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Franklin Templeton is teaming up with MoonPay to let big investors swap stablecoins for yields 24/7
items.subheadline: The Wall Street giant is integrating its tech platform with MoonPay's infrastructure, allowing eligible institutions to seamlessly swap stablecoins for yield-generating tokenized funds without ever leaving the blockchain.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Why the Ethereum Foundation is suddenly again at the center of crypto’s culture war
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of the Protocol Newsletter, we're diving deep into the institution that has been the main steward for the Ethereum blockchain, and why its been back in the spotlight.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Solana, Sui and Aptos wallet data targeted in TrapDoor package attack
items.subheadline: The campaign targets crypto, DeFi, AI and security developers with fake tooling packages to steal wallets, SSH keys, GitHub tokens, cloud credentials and browser data.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Mass deployment of AI agents is a disaster waiting to happen, says CertiK CEO
items.subheadline: Ronghui Gu shares tips on how to isolate AI agents while testing them so they do not have access to critical personal information or digital assets.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Bitcoin’s biggest quantum risk may not be wallet keys. An early investor fears something bigger
items.subheadline: Andrew Gault, the venture capitalist who funded the quantum hardware labs now threatening bitcoin, says the industry is looking in the wrong place. Google's own security team moved in the same direction in March.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP Ledger's design blocks the flash loan attacks costing DeFi hundreds of millions
items.subheadline: A draft XRPL amendment notes that flash loan attacks are "structurally impossible" on the network because of how its transactions are built, an architectural quirk that has spared the chain from the exploit class that has cost Ethereum DeFi billions.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Three Sui mainnet halts in 48 hours traced to an upgrade bug by developers
items.subheadline: The Sui Foundation's post-mortem published Sunday traces all three outages to interactions between a new address-balance feature shipped in the v1.72 release and the network's existing gas and consensus logic.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Whitehat developer unlocks $2 million stuck in a 2016 Ethereum ICO contract for nine years
items.subheadline: 0xflorent, a security researcher, found an integer-overflow flaw in the HongCoin token sale contract that lets the team unlock funds for 48 original investors. It is the second such recovery he has publicized in eight days.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum's Vitalik Buterin is rethinking how DeFi handles market crashes
items.subheadline: In a research post published Monday, Buterin proposed creating index-tracking assets using options contracts rather than the debt-based structures that underpin much of DeFi today.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Movement pivots to stablecoin payments as the layer-2 boom loses momentum
items.subheadline: The team behind Movement said it plans to leverage licensed payment partners alongside blockchain settlement rails to target the roughly $685 billion remittance market serving low and middle-income countries.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: The time is now: the Senate must act on crypto market structure legislation
items.subheadline: The United States needs to finally establish the clear framework that the market needs.
items.author: Summer Mersinger

items.headline: How DeFi is changing the financial landscape for Latin Americans
items.subheadline: DeFi is quietly shifting from niche crypto experiment to a legitimate financial tool across the region, explains Serrano.
items.author: Sebastián Serrano

items.headline: It’s time for clarity for America’s digital asset markets
items.subheadline: Recent polls have shown that registered voters want America to set the rules for global finance, reinforcing why the Senate must act now, explains Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Iran war shows markets no longer sleep
items.subheadline: The latest conflict involving Iran has produced an unexpected proving ground for financial infrastructure, and an unlikely winner has emerged, argues Huang.
items.author: Annabelle Huang

items.headline: The U.S. can’t lose the bitcoin race to China
items.subheadline: The next global power competition is not being fought over missiles alone. It’s being fought over money, and right now, China is moving aggressively to shape the future of it, argues Gooden.
items.author: Congressman Lance Gooden

items.headline: The agentic CFO in your pocket
items.subheadline: Chalom explains that retail investors have never had the opportunity to access and manage their own digital treasury desk. Until now.
items.author: Joseph Chalom

items.headline: The Clarity Act won’t lead to adoption without crypto tax reform
items.subheadline: The legislation is being framed as a turning point for U.S. crypto policy, but there’s a major piece of the puzzle being overlooked, argues Singh.
items.author: Robin Singh

items.headline: White House reviews CFTC prediction-market rule as Trump backs federal control
items.subheadline: The proposal could shape how Kalshi, Polymarket, and other event-contract platforms operate as states challenge the CFTC’s authority.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: Kalshi follows CFTC in suing Minnesota over its law criminalizing prediction markets
items.subheadline: The Governor of Minnesota signed into law a measure that starting August 1 makes it a crime to advertise and operate prediction market platforms across the state.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Paxos wins SEC approval to clear U.S. stocks on blockchain
items.subheadline: Paxos new license positions it alongside legacy giants such as DTCC and makes it a compelling, more efficient alternative for traditional finance giants than legacy competitors.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: U.S. CFTC opens crypto 'perp' door with first approval at regulated firm
items.subheadline: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission has now established a stance on how regulated U.S. firms can engage in crypto perpetual futures contracts.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Clarity Act Risks Regulation Without Oversight, Brookings Fellow Says
items.subheadline: As Congress weighs crypto legislation, Aaron Klein says the CFTC needs more resources, independence and coordination to oversee digital markets.
items.author: AI Boost

items.headline: U.S. regulator says 24/7 trading is great for crypto, may not be fit for other sectors
items.subheadline: As the CFTC issued landmark approvals for crypto perpetual futures contracts, it explained in a related advisory that round-the-clock activity isn't right for all.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: ‘The banks will not accept it’: JP Morgan's Dimon escalates battle over stablecoin rewards in CLARITY Act debate
items.subheadline: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon criticized Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong and warned the current CLARITY Act framework could ultimately fail, as banks and crypto firms clash over whether stablecoin issuers should be allowed to offer yield-bearing rewards that resemble bank deposits.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) faces an 'identity crisis' and DeFi devs need to stop acting like tech bros
items.subheadline: Solstice Labs CEO Ben Nadareski says developers must act like financial managers to win back institutional trust amid ongoing security exploits.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Bitcoin price news: BTC may face deeper losses as capital chases AI stocks, K33 says
items.subheadline: The firm still views bitcoin as undervalued relative to equities, but says investors are rotating into AI stocks as the opportunity cost of missing gains by holding BTC is too high.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Tom Lee predicts ETH will hit $250,000 as corporate validators take over network control
items.subheadline: The Bitmine chairman said DeFi and AI could push the Ethereum network's value into the multi-trillion range, making current prices “future optionality at a discount”.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Galaxy enters institutional prediction markets with $10 million Arca trade
items.subheadline: The digital asset firm launched OTC prediction markets trading for institutions and completed a $10 million trade tied to U.S. crypto legislation with hedge fund Arca.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Coinbase (COIN) backs Ethena (ENA) ahead of savings product launch for 100 million users
items.subheadline: Coinbase Ventures, the exchange's venture arm, bought Ethena tokens as the protocol is set to roll out a Coinbase integration next week.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: U.S. sanctions Nobitex, other Iranian crypto exchanges amid ongoing war
items.subheadline: OFAC said it was blacklisting Nobitex and three other Iranian exchanges, as well as a few executives, citing links to terror and other crimes.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: UK House of Lords committee calls on Bank of England to reconsider proposed stablecoin restrictions
items.subheadline: The Bank of England proposed limits of 20,000 pounds per coin for individuals and 10 million pounds for businesses.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Bitcoin’s compute power dwarfs top 100 supercomputers by 600k times, says Bittensor co-founder
items.subheadline: Ala Shaabana explains how using Bitcoin network’s coordinate-and-reward playbook can bust open corporate monopolies on AI.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Bitcoin's slide to $66,000 is accelerating a shift into digital dollars
items.subheadline: The crypto market is seeing a capital flight into dollar-linked stablecoins even as stocks and the Dollar Index remain calm.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Mastercard expands onchain settlement in bet on stablecoins and always-on finance
items.subheadline: The company plans to offer stablecoin, weekend and holiday settlement as demand grows for real-time movement of money.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: BTC price news: What next as Bitcoin falls below $66,000
items.subheadline: BTC plunged 6.4% to a 24-hour low of $65,708 and ether broke below $1,900 in Asian trading on Wednesday, just hours after the MSCI All Country World Index set a fresh all-time high on the AI rally.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bullish crypto bets lose $1.6 billion as ETH, SOL, DOGE drop 9%
items.subheadline: The single biggest unwind was a $59.67 million BTC-USDT long on HTX.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: XRP price news: Ripple-linked token drops 5% even as bullish signals pile up
items.subheadline: XRP lost another major support level on heavy selling, with traders now focused on whether buyers can defend the $1.20 area after a 15-week low.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin's 'fear gauge' surges nearly 20%, its biggest jump since Feb. 5 crash
items.subheadline: The jump signals return of fear after two months of calm market sentiment.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Prediction market traders bet bitcoin's selloff has further to run
items.subheadline: Markets now imply a 66% chance bitcoin falls below $55,000 and a coin-flip chance of sub-$50,000 prices before year-end.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: Big tech is 'terrified' of AI agents wiping out ad revenue, says Billions Network CEO
items.subheadline: Evin McMullen’s view on AI agents disrupting Google’s and Facebook’s business model was previously shared by Cardano Founder Charles Hoskinson and Cloudflare CSO Stephanie Cohen.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Franklin Templeton says Wall Street fears blockchain because it threatens its profits
items.subheadline: Jenny Johnson, Franklin Templeton's CEO, said blockchain and crypto threaten a huge number of business models that exist today in traditional finance.
items.author: Olivier Acuna
